headline: Chip Gear and Glass Lead a Cautious Rally as Geopolitical Calm Lifts Risk Appetite
body_html:
Wall Street is pressing modestly higher at midday, with 103 of the 200 names in our tracked universe advancing against 97 decliners — a narrow but positive tape. The dominant theme is a relief rally tied to the reported pause in U.S.-Iran hostilities, which has steadied sentiment across technology and energy while pulling some safe-haven demand out of gold. Semiconductors are the clear sector leader, up roughly 2.1%, driven by outsized moves in semiconductor-equipment names.

The standout movers of the session are concentrated in hardware and connectivity. Roblox has surged more than 14% amid broader enthusiasm for internet platforms, while Corning has added nearly 14% on heavy volume. Western Digital and the two leading semi-equipment names — Applied Materials and KLA Corp — are each up more than 10%, underscoring the market's appetite for capital-equipment plays tied to data-infrastructure spending. Bloom Energy rounds out the top gainers with a gain approaching 9%, extending the Energy sector's 0.85% advance.

On the downside, steel names Steel Dynamics and Nucor are off sharply, and homebuilding-adjacent names such as Weyerhaeuser and Martin Marietta are dragging on those sectors. Consumer and Homebuilders sit at the bottom of the sector ladder. Into the close, traders will monitor any fresh headlines from U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks in Doha and pre-position ahead of Friday's jobs report, which prediction markets suggest may underperform consensus expectations.

briefs.headline: Ceasefire Signal Lifts Broad Equities, Though Gains Remain Tentative
briefs.body: Major indexes are climbing after reports that the United States and Iran have agreed to halt attacks, with diplomatic envoys set to travel to Doha for follow-on talks. The move has reduced near-term risk premiums across equities, though market breadth remains thin, suggesting conviction is still cautious ahead of further geopolitical developments.

briefs.headline: Semi-Equipment Names Surge on Data-Infrastructure Demand Thesis
briefs.body: Applied Materials and KLA Corp each gained more than 10% through midday, propelling the Semiconductors sector to a 2.05% advance — the best performance among all tracked sectors. Analysts point to sustained capital spending on AI-related fabrication capacity as the underlying catalyst sustaining demand for front-end equipment suppliers.

briefs.headline: Corning Shares Vault Higher on Connectivity Optimism
briefs.body: Corning posted a gain approaching 14% by midday, making it the second-largest mover in the universe today. The specialty-glass maker has benefited from growing appetite for optical and fiber infrastructure as data-center buildout accelerates, with the Materials-Glass segment outperforming the broader Materials sector's already-solid 0.84% advance.

briefs.headline: Steel Sector Hit Hard as Construction Outlook Dims
briefs.body: Steel Dynamics fell more than 4.5% and Nucor shed nearly 3.7% midday, placing both among the session's sharpest decliners. Weakness in Homebuilders, down 0.81%, and a softening construction-spending narrative appear to be weighing on steel demand expectations, cutting against any benefit from the broader geopolitical relief rally.

briefs.headline: Prediction Markets Flag Jobs-Report Risk Ahead of Friday's Release
briefs.body: Traders on Kalshi's event contracts are assigning meaningful probability to a below-consensus payrolls print this Friday, a signal that is already shaping hedging activity late in the week. A disappointing number could reintroduce rate-path uncertainty and test the durability of today's rally heading into the long quarter-end weekend.

briefs.headline: Middle East Oil Flow Continues Despite Regional Tension
briefs.body: Energy producers in the Middle East are maintaining crude and liquefied natural gas shipments despite ongoing security risks from ship attacks, according to Reuters. The Energy sector is up 0.85% midday, with fuel-cell specialist Bloom Energy adding nearly 9%, reflecting both the supply-continuity signal and sustained interest in alternative energy infrastructure.
